codeReview.summary: The feature adds quote_decimals plumbing and broad 8-decimal tests, but market registration trusts a caller-provided quote_decimals value without validating it against the actual quote asset metadata decimals, which can create misconfigured markets and incorrect client price encoding.

issues.description: register_market only range-checks quote_decimals (1..18) and stores it, but never verifies it matches the quote asset's real metadata decimals. A mismatched value (e.g., passing 6 for an 8-decimal FA) will publish incorrect market metadata and can cause downstream clients to encode prices incorrectly. Evidence: range check at line 98 and immediate use of caller input at lines 101-116; no fungible_asset::decimals validation in this module.

In test_8dec_gtc_limit_buy, comments say price is 2.0 USD1 and locked amount is 200 USD1, but the encoded test price is 2_000_000, which computes to 200_000_000 quote subunits (2 USD1 at 8 decimals). The assertion is correct for 2 USD1, but comments are inconsistent and can mislead future decimal-encoding changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The move-worker skill still states quote precision assumptions as fixed 6-decimal USDC/CASH, which is now stale after this feature introduced configurable quote_decimals.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/move-worker/SKILL.md:37 ('CASH and USDC both have 6 decimals. PRICE_SCALE = 1_000_000.') and line 89 ('Unsure about decimal precision... USDC=6 decimals'). Handoff skillFeedback also flags this needed update.

summary: Reviewed feature contract-decimal-flexibility (commit 9e67813) via handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and diff. Core decimal support was added, but registration lacks a metadata-decimals consistency check, so this review is marked fail with one blocking issue.
